compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can walk ticket hierarchies via the CLI
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket list-descendants <id>` subcommand that performs a BFS tree walk from the given ticket and returns a JSON object keyed by tier name (epics, stories, tasks, bugs), each value an array of descendant IDs at that tier.

**Why**: Enables callers to traverse epic-story-task hierarchies through a stable CLI contract, without reimplementing BFS over .tickets-tracker/ layout. Foundation for multiple downstream workflow queries.

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-list-descendants.sh (or Python) implementation, dispatcher integration, BFS traversal, JSON output keyed by tier, integration test asserting BFS ordering and correct tier bucketing, unit tests
- OUT: Migrating callers to use this subcommand (that is Epic e1b2-2653's concern, if applicable)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list-descendants <id>` returns a JSON object whose keys are tier names (epics, stories, tasks, bugs) and whose values are arrays of descendant ticket IDs discovered via BFS
  ← Satisfies: "list-descendants (BFS tree walk returning child IDs by tier as a JSON object keyed by tier name)"
- When this story is complete, an integration test verifies that the BFS walk discovers descendants across multiple tiers on a synthetic fixture and groups them into the correct tier buckets
  ← Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] BFS implementation should reuse ticket-reducer.py's hierarchy walk helpers if available, rather than reimplementing
- [Testing] Fixture should include multi-level hierarchies (epic → story → task) to exercise BFS tier bucketing

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Output must expose BOTH (a) the tier-keyed descendant lists AND (b) the parents-with-children predicate (equivalent to sprint-next-batch.sh's parent_ids_with_children.txt). Concrete schema option: add a top-level 'parents_with_children: [id, ...]' array, OR provide per-node has_children booleans. Consumers (specifically the next-batch subcommand, story 958a-66ac) distinguish 'parent story' from 'story needing planning' using this predicate to preserve SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING behavior. Without this predicate, next-batch cannot produce correct SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING output. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
